postTitle: Spring Cleaning Your Cloud: Past the Quick Wins Into the Hard Questions

short.text:
Cloud waste hit 29%. Trending up for the first time in five years.

Most FinOps conversations stop at orphaned resources and oversized VMs. Necessary. Not sufficient.

The real question for mature customers: what are we choosing to spend money on, and is that choice still justified?

I wrote about the four lenses that turn a FinOps review into an architecture conversation.

Which cost conversation is your team avoiding?

medium.text:
Cloud waste hit 29% this year. Trending up for the first time in five years.

Most FinOps conversations stop at the quick wins: orphaned resources, oversized VMs, dev/test running 24/7. Necessary. Not sufficient.

For mature customers, the real question is not "where is the waste". It is "what are we choosing to spend money on, and is that choice still justified".

I structure the hard conversation around four lenses:

1. Cost of architecture choices — what are we paying for because of design?
2. Cost of product choices — what are we paying for because of what the business asked for?
3. Cost of organizational choices — what are we paying for because ownership is unclear?
4. Cost of inertia — what are we paying for because change is harder than overspend?

Which of those four would make the most uncomfortable conversation at your company?
